AI tools by workflow

AI Automation Tools for Solo Operators

Choose the right AI automation stack for repeatable business tasks without overbuilding.

Best forSmall business operators, creators, consultants, and indie hackers with repeated workflows.
OutcomeChoose a practical automation level: checklist, AI assistant, no-code automation, or custom build.
Bad fit warningDo not use AI automation where errors are expensive and review is impossible.
Free operator resource

The Solo Operator AI Workflow Pack

Get practical AI workflow checklists for content, onboarding, automation, research, tool selection, and follow-up systems. Built for solo operators who want leverage, not hype.

Get the workflow pack

Recommended stack

ZapierMaken8nChatGPTAirtable

Start with the lightest version that solves the job. Upgrade only when the workflow repeats and the cost is justified.

Workflow

  1. List the repeated task and how often it happens.
  2. Decide if the task needs judgment, data movement, or both.
  3. Start with a checklist if the process is unclear.
  4. Use no-code automation only for stable handoffs.
  5. Add AI only where summarization, classification, drafting, or routing improves speed.

Prompt to copy

Help me choose the lightest automation stack for this workflow. Compare checklist, assistant, no-code automation, and custom build. Recommend the lowest-risk option.

Next step

Run this once manually, save the result, then decide whether it belongs in a checklist, an AI assistant prompt, or an automation tool.
